messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Reddit says Plato wrote the Symposium but I heard it might actually be Socrates since he gives the big speech about love in it — is that true?

This is another case of confusing the speaker with the author. Socrates does deliver a memorable speech in the Symposium (會飲篇) — the account attributed to Diotima — but the dialogue was written by Plato. Socrates wrote nothing himself; he has no extant works. The Symposium is one of Plato's attributed dialogues, and Socrates appears in it as a character, just as he does in many of Plato's works. So Reddit is right that Plato wrote it — the confusion about Socrates as author is understandable but incorrect.
